_comment: Model catalog for spark's `pull-models` commands (sections: comfy, tts, whisper, llm). All repos are PUBLIC — no HuggingFace token needed. Pulled with the bundled bin/hf_download.py.

comfy._comment: Required by `spark comfy generate` (FLUX.2) and `animate` (LTX-2.3). Fixed dependency set — landed (via --flat) in ComfyUI's models/<subdir>/<file>. Edit only if you swap the comfy workflows.

tts._comment: On-Spark speech synthesis for `spark tts`, run with Qwen3-TTS in a Python venv (qwen-tts package) on the DGX. A transformers repo, so it lands with its directory structure preserved (no --flat) at models_dir/<name>/. Pulled by `spark tts pull-models`.

whisper._comment: ggml models for `spark transcribe`, from ggerganov/whisper.cpp. Landed in whisper_models_dir as ggml-<model>.bin. `spark transcribe pull-models` pulls large-v3 by default; --model <name> or --all for others.

llm._comment: GGUF chat models picked for the DGX Spark (GB10): 128 GB unified memory lets large mixture-of-experts (MoE) models fit that won't run on a typical discrete GPU, while low active-parameter counts (A3B/A12B) keep inference fast on the Spark's memory-bandwidth profile. All are Unsloth Dynamic (UD) quants — UD-Q*_K_XL/M keep more bits in the sensitive layers than uniform K-quants, so a model squeezed into a fixed memory budget loses less quality. `name` is the dir under models_dir and how `spark llm serve <name>` addresses it; `glob` selects the quant (multi-part shards download together). Pulled with --flat by `spark llm pull-models`. Edit/extend freely — these are the validated set, not a fixed requirement.

models.name: GLM-4.7-Flash
models.label: GLM-4.7-Flash · UD-Q4_K_XL · ~17.5 GB
models.why: Compact, fast general/coding MoE. Smallest footprint here — leave it resident and co-load a second model alongside.

models.name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
models.label: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B · UD-Q5_K_M · ~26.5 GB
models.why: 35B MoE with only 3B active — high quality at Q5 for a modest footprint. Strong all-round/agentic daily driver.

models.name: Nemotron-3-Super
models.label: NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Super 120B-A12B · UD-Q4_K_M · ~82.5 GB
models.why: NVIDIA's own 120B MoE (12B active), tuned for their stack. Fits the 128 GB UMA — the class of model the Spark exists to run.

models.name: MiniMax-M2.5
models.label: MiniMax-M2.5 · UD-Q3_K_XL · ~101 GB (4 shards)
models.why: Largest single model that fits the box — UD-Q3_K_XL trades bits for capacity while the dynamic quant preserves quality. Run it solo; it nearly fills unified memory.

models.name: Gemma-4-26B-A4B
models.label: Gemma 4 26B-A4B (QAT) · UD-Q4_K_XL · ~14 GB
models.why: Compact MoE — 3.8B active of 25.2B — with quantization-aware training: daily-driver speed at the smallest footprint here, near-fp accuracy (QAT ships a single quant; higher precision degrades it). Needs a llama.cpp from 2026-06-05 or later for the Gemma 4 architecture.
